title: Homepage Component
content:
This file defines the main landing page for the DocMed application. It utilizes various UI components to present the product's value proposition, features, and a call to action.

## Purpose

The primary purpose of this file is to serve as the entry point for the application's homepage, showcasing the core features and benefits of DocMed to potential users.

## Key Components & Exports

- **`Navbar`**: A sticky header component with navigation links and a mobile menu toggle.
- **`Hero`**: The main hero section featuring a large headline, subtext, and call-to-action buttons, visually enhanced with a globe component.
- **`TrustBar`**: A horizontal bar displaying key technologies and integrations.
- **`Features`**: A section detailing the core features of DocMed, each with an icon, title, description, and a highlight.
- **`HowItWorks`**: An illustrative section explaining the three-step process of using DocMed.
- **`Stats`**: A section presenting key statistics and achievements related to DocMed's effectiveness.
- **`CTA` (Call to Action)**: A prominent section encouraging users to sign up, featuring an email input and a "Get Early Access" button.
- **`Footer`**: A standard footer with the company logo and copyright information.
- **`Home`**: The main exported React component that orchestrates all the above sections.

## Usage Example

This file is the root component for the application's homepage and is typically rendered by the Next.js routing system when a user navigates to the root URL (`/`).

```tsx
// In app/page.tsx
import Home from './page';

// ... other Next.js page logic

export default Home;
```
